name: braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks
summary: BrakTooth Proof of Concept Attacks (+ BT Fuzzing Framework)

arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

examples: sudo apt install -y unzip python3-dev python3-venv
python3 firmware.py flash /dev/ttyUSB1 # Please change your serial port to match your ESP32 device.
sudo apt install -y zstd wget unzip
sudo bin/bt_fuzzer --scan # Scan for targets (BDAddress) for 15 seconds

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ukBaz/python-bluezero (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with mackowiakd/BLE-Security-PoC (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with hbldh/bleak (score=11); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: BrakTooth Proof of Concept Attacks (+ BT Fuzzing Framework).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: BrakTooth Proof of Concept Attacks (+ BT Fuzzing Framework).
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo apt install -y unzip python3-dev python3-venv`; `unzip esp32driver.zip # Extract esp32driver.zip (firmware package)`. BrakTooth Proof of Concept Attacks (+ BT Fuzzing Framework).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks because BrakTooth Proof of Concept Attacks (+ BT Fuzzing Framework).
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo apt install -y unzip python3-dev python3-venv` as documented upstream for braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `unzip esp32driver.zip # Extract esp32driver.zip (firmware package)` as documented upstream for braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 firmware.py flash /dev/ttyUSB1 # Please change your serial port to match your ESP32 device.` as documented upstream for braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `braktooth_esp32_bluetooth_classic_attacks --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: sudo apt install -y unzip python3-dev python3-venv
python3 firmware.py flash /dev/ttyUSB1 # Please change your serial port to match your ESP32 device.
sudo apt install -y zstd wget unzip
sudo bin/bt_fuzzer --scan # Scan for targets (BDAddress) for 15 seconds
